checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2014-12-31 value=50057803.71
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2013-12-31 value=38258349.87

evidence.location: 10-Q Q1-2015 balance sheet and income statement

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end=2012-12-31 value=745766616.73 vs end=2011-12-31 value=653673811.86 (+14.1%)
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end=2015-03-31 value=919911501.09 vs end=2014-12-31 value=853251351.23 (+7.8% in one quarter)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss start=2014-01-01 end=2014-12-31 value=64371231.99
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations start=2014-01-01 end=2014-12-31 value=68495161.55 vs start=2013-01-01 end=2013-12-31 value=85619268.02 (-20.0% while NI grew +19.3%)
NetIncomeLoss start=2015-01-01 end=2015-03-31 value=26484183.28 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations start=2015-01-01 end=2015-03-31 value=13935805.29

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfGoodsSold concept present in the provided financial_series_point_in_time dataset

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss start=2015-01-01 end=2015-03-31 value=26484183.28 vs OperatingIncomeLoss start=2015-01-01 end=2015-03-31 value=21591942.68 and InterestExpense start=2015-01-01 end=2015-03-31 value=8975289.10 (implied post-interest operating earnings ~12.6M, far below reported NI)
NetIncomeLoss start=2014-01-01 end=2014-03-31 value=25189475.92 is markedly higher than subsequent quarters: Q2=14885341.65, Q3=14236302.15, Q4=10060112.27

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable or similar reserve concept present in the dataset

DeferredRevenueCurrent end=2012-12-31 value=3816267.98 rising to end=2013-12-31 value=12275275.66 then declining to end=2014-12-31 value=10527083.55, an inconsistent trend not clearly tied to revenue growth

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2015-04-30 amending 10-K filing_date=2015-03-02
LongTermDebtCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=280734450.09 versus end=2014-03-31 value=25814071.07 and end=2014-09-30 value=25760546.39 (one-quarter reclassification spike)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spans from 2007-12-31 through 2015-03-31 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, cash flow statement items, and balance sheet items, covering ~7 years and multiple filing types (10-K, 10-K/A, 10-Q, 10-Q/A)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., recognizing revenue ahead of cash collection or extending credit terms/channel stuffing), causing receivables to build up disproportionately to reported sales, most acutely in FY2014 (AR +30.8% vs revenue +6.6%) and Q1 2015 (AR +5.8% while revenue fell 17.9%).

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2014-12-31 value=50057803.71 vs end=2013-12-31 value=38258349.87 (+30.8%)
Revenues start=2015-01-01 end=2015-03-31 value=40201253.83 vs start=2014-01-01 end=2014-03-31 value=48946850.39 (-17.9%) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent rose from 49647307.30 to 52509402.92

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Earnings growth outpacing and diverging from operating cash flow, and a large unexplained gap between operating income (net of interest) and reported net income in Q1 2015, suggests non-cash/non-operating gains (e.g., fair-value/mark-to-market adjustments, gain on debt extinguishment, or tax benefits) or aggressive accrual recognition inflating net income relative to underlying cash generation.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss FY2014=64371231.99 (+19.3% YoY) while NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations FY2014=68495161.55 declined -20.0% from FY2013=85619268.02

NetIncomeLoss Q1-2015=26484183.28 exceeds OperatingIncomeLoss Q1-2015=21591942.68 minus InterestExpense Q1-2015=8975289.10 (implied post-interest operating earnings ~12.6M), and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations Q1-2015 was only 13935805.29

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Extended, multi-quarter SEC comment-letter correspondence (CORRESP/UPLOAD) through 2013-2015 culminating in a 10-K/A amendment of the FY2014 Form 10-K, combined with an anomalous one-quarter reclassification of ~$280M of long-term debt to current in Q2 2014 before reverting to ~$26M in Q3 2014, suggests possible balance-sheet classification or disclosure issues under active SEC review that could affect reported liability and/or intangible asset presentation.

rationale_evidence.quote: LongTermDebtCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=280734450.09 vs end=2014-03-31 value=25814071.07 and end=2014-09-30 value=25760546.39
10-K/A filing_date=2015-04-30 following 10-K filing_date=2015-03-02 amid sustained CORRESP/UPLOAD filings from 2013-11-25 through 2015-07-20

top_signals: Receivables grew far faster than revenue in FY2014 (+30.8% vs +6.6%) and diverged sharply in Q1 2015 (AR up while revenue fell ~18%)

Net income grew while operating cash flow declined in FY2014, and Q1 2015 net income (26.5M) nearly double operating cash flow (13.9M), with a large unexplained gap versus operating income net of interest

Extensive, multi-quarter SEC comment letter correspondence (2013-2015) culminating in a 10-K/A amendment of the FY2014 10-K, alongside an anomalous one-quarter spike in current portion of long-term debt
